full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Figuring out how to honestly compare different-dataset results.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: i got 100% on iris but paper reports like 87% on sonar. my exam says to compare my result to the paper's but they're on totally different datasets. how do i write this without making it look like i failed
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — writing guidance. All text is my own.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested framing it around validating the qualitative claim (offset-free ≈ standard SVM accuracy) rather than matching exact numbers. That matched what the exam instructions say about not presenting gaps as failures.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-read the exam instructions which say 'describe it as an honest observation'.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: should i plot a confusion matrix or just show accuracy as a number for the reproducibility section
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Chose confusion matrix over bar chart. Plotting code is mine.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Said confusion matrix is better because it shows per-class breakdown, not just aggregate accuracy. That's more informative for a binary classifier.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Saved the plot to results/ as required.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Quick check on reproducibility checklist format.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: what does hyperparameters in one place mean for the reproducibility checklist, do i just put them in a dict at the top?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I added a HYPERPARAMS section at the top of the notebook. Structure is my own.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed yes, a dict or labelled constants at the top is the standard approach so someone can change values without hunting through code.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Reviewed the notebook to make sure all hyperparameter uses point to the top-of-file definitions.
